name: Chicken and Vegetable Terrine
recipeIngredient: 3/4 pound haricots verts, parboiled for 5 minutes
1 clove garlic cooked until tender in
1/4 cup Champagne or white wine vinegar
Salt and freshly ground black pepper
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F and lightly grease an 8 x 4 inch glass loaf pan.
2. Cut carrots into strips, each strip measuring 1/4 inch wide.
3. Parboil carrots for 6-7 minutes until cooked through but still with texture.
4. In a food processor, puree ground chicken with oil, vinegar, salt, and pepper.
5. Add cooked shallots, garlic, bread crumbs, and egg; continue pureeing until smooth.
6. Divide the chicken puree into three bowls:
- Add carrots to one bowl
- Add green beans to another
- Leave plain in a third bowl
7. Mix vegetables thoroughly into their respective chicken mixtures.
8. Place a small amount of plain chicken puree at the bottom of the loaf pan and spread evenly.
9. Cover with carrot layer, keeping carrots straight.
10. Layer green bean mixture over carrots.
11. Add a thin layer of chicken binder on top of green beans.
12. Lay black olives down the center; dot with additional chicken binder.
13. Repeat green bean layer, then top with carrot layer and final chicken binder layer.
14. Cover with buttered brown paper, pressing directly onto terrine.
15. Place foil over the top and bake in a larger pan filled with boiling water up to 3/4 of the loaf pan's height for 1 hour.
16. Remove from oven; let rest another hour or until cool.
17. Leave foil cover on while draining excess water.
18. Run knife between terrine and pan, then carefully turn upside down onto a board.
19. Pat dry with paper towels.
20. Wrap in plastic wrap and foil, then refrigerate for 3 hours or overnight.
Yield: 6 to 8 servings
